description_long: This is a rounded, double-handled ceramic vase with a wide mouth and a slightly tapered base. The surface is adorned with intricate geometric patterns in earthy tones of reddish-brown and beige, giving it an ancient or traditional appearance. The handles are robust and smoothly integrated into the body, providing both decorative and functional value. The rim is thick and slightly flared, while the body features a series of repeating linear and angular motifs. The finish appears matte and slightly rough, suggesting it was hand-crafted and possibly aged. The overall form and decoration evoke a sense of historical or artisanal craftsmanship, suitable for display in a public or decorative urban setting.
description: A rounded, double-handled ceramic vase with geometric patterns in earthy tones and a matte, slightly rough finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both handles and the most detailed geometric patterns on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one handle and the continuous pattern wrapping around the vase.
description_view_2: Rear view with both handles visible and a central circular motif between them.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with one handle and a less detailed section of the pattern.
